publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 02:19:51 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ezra Barhight was a lumberman in Potter County, Pennsylvania. Ellen Stekert is a member of the Cornell University Class of 1957 and was a professor of folklore at Wayne State University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Tape recordings of English, Irish, and American folk tunes and popular songs sung by Ezra Barhight and recorded by Edith Fox and Ellen Stekert. The songs recorded when Barhight was in his 80s. Barhight learned some songs when his parents cooked for and took care of a lumber camp and his mother would sing them. Other songs he would learn later from rafters and loggers when he was working as a lumberman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: DVD-1492 contains digital copies of some of the material.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the donor.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ezra Barhight. Tape Recordings, #1721. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: TR-568-570.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Edith M. Fox. Includes: The Clock; Let's Go Hunting; Roger O'Hare; Dam Commol (Erin Go Braugh); The Darby Ram; Five Dollar Note; Give My Love to Nellie, Jack; The Daddy of Them All (Michael Fennegan); Finnegan's Wake; Mamcy's Murder - Pat O'Brien; Johnny the Sailor - The Green Bed; Doran's Ass; Looking for the Ball (Stonedam); Rafting Song; Jack Haggerty; Old Armchair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert. Includes: The Flying Cloud; Little Brother Joe; Andy Bardee; The Devil and the Farmer's Wife (Curst Wife); In Search of Maria; The Ship That Never Returned; Don't Be So Heartless and Cruel; State of Arkansas; Hills of Glenshee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert 3-11-1956, includes: Oh, Do Not Be Offended Please; And She Never Came Back; And This is What She Ate; Johnny Bull; A Man A Little Bit Strange; The Piper and the Cow; Laurina; Another Fellow In This Town (Looks Like Me); Brennan on the Moor; I'll Not Go Out with Riley Anymore; McSorley's Most Beautiful Twins. Recorded 3-14-1956: Bright-Eyed Laughing Little Nell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert. Includes: Charming Young Widow I Met on the Train; Black Cook; He Followed Her By Night (Lady Isabel and The Elf Knight); Mary of the Wild Moor; Fair Ellen (The Jealous Lover); Bounding the United States; Abe Lincoln Went to Washington; When Johnny Comes Marching Home; Texas Rangers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert. Includes: Lovely Minnisoty; A Paddy in Ireland; Johnny Troy; Old Grey; Never Take the Horse Shoe From The Door; Willie Weever; You are Nothing More to Me; Little Cottage - Auctioneer Stop That Sale; huckleberry Dew Cumberland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert. Includes: The Girl I left in Ireland; Toll, Toll, the Bell (Brith Eyed Laughing Little Nell); By Borders and by Oceans; Under the Daisy; Two Sisters; The Western Pioneers; You Made Your Dear Old Mother Weep; The Drummer Boy; The 20th of July; She's More To be Pitied than Scorned At; The Hills of New Hampshire; Father You Will Marry (The Blind Girl); Buying of the Fox.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded by Ellen Stekert. Includes: The Fox; One Morning in May; Give Me a Home by the Sea; Green Fields of Virginia; Irish Jaunting Car; I Have No Home.
